title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government is responding to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, likely addressing key findings and recommendations to shape AI policy. The response aims to outline the government's stance on implementing or refining AI standards in the public sector.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This policy document outlines the Department for Education's stance on using generative artificial intelligence in educational settings. It likely covers the potential benefits and risks of incorporating generative AI into the education sector.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications. It responds to requests from various UK government ministers to inform their understanding of AI use in qualifications.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report examines the relationship between copyright law and the development of artificial intelligence, exploring the implications of using copyright works to train AI systems. It assesses the potential impact of copyright law on the development and use of AI technology.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report from the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, outlining its plans and actions to address concerns raised about the use of AI in the country. The response aims to ensure that AI is safely and beneficially integrated into UK society.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This AI policy document outlines the scope and responsibilities of a committee tasked with reviewing the relationship between artificial intelligence and public standards. It provides an overview of the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for the committee's review.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office's Centre for Safer and More Secure Information (CSPL) has provided evidence on the governance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to a government committee. The submission likely discusses how AI should be managed and governed to ensure its safe and secure development.

title: Impact of AI on the regulation of medical products
description: Implementing the Artificial Intelligence (AI) White Paper principles.
summary: This document discusses the impact of AI on the regulations of medical products. It aims to implement the principles outlined in the AI White Paper to inform these regulations.

title: Defence Artificial Intelligence Strategy
description: Our vision is that, in terms of AI, we will be the world’s most effective, efficient, trusted and influential Defence organisation for our size.
summary: The Defence Artificial Intelligence Strategy outlines a vision for the UK government's defence organisation to become the most effective and influential in AI. The goal is to achieve this through AI, aiming for efficiency, trust, and influence in the defence area.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document, titled "Transport artificial intelligence action plan", outlines the UK Department for Transport's strategy for using artificial intelligence in the transport sector, balancing benefits and potential risks. It aims to work collaboratively with the transport sector to harness AI opportunities while managing challenges.
